reasoning: The reference class is Trump fulfilling his tariff promises within the 2025 calendar year. Initial evidence showed Trump implemented massive baseline, reciprocal, and retaliatory tariffs under IEEPA and Section 232 earlier in 2025. Although a U.S. court issued an injunction blocking the IEEPA-based baseline and reciprocal tariffs on May 28-29, 2025, the Yale Budget Lab still calculated the estimated effective tariff rate for to-date non-invalidated/delayed tariffs at 6.9% as of May 29 (down from an initial >20%). Furthermore, Trump has multiple fallback legal authorities (like Section 301 and 232) that he is already reportedly looking to use as workarounds to restore the blocked duties. Because only one quarter needs to hit the 6% threshold across all of 2025, the high effective rate prior to the late-May injunctions could be enough to push Q2 average past 6%, or he will likely succeed in implementing alternative substantial tariffs in Q3/Q4. The late-May market forecast was 0.93; incorporating the court roadblocks slightly cools the certainty but still leans heavily YES given the remaining active tariffs and alternative options.
